[2018-03-08 00:18] http404error: Cave Story, free action platformer
[2018-03-08 00:19] mqp: if anyone wants to play me CEO in some way where i don't have to grind to get strong pieces or something i will happily play
[2018-03-08 00:19] mqp: i don't know if that way exists
[2018-03-08 00:19] ryanfourpm: I think if the CEO developer wasn't MIA half the time CEO could become a pretty great game
[2018-03-08 00:19] ryanfourpm: there is some grinding in CEO yeah
[2018-03-08 00:20] http404error: Clearly, someone should make a better CEO then
[2018-03-08 00:20] lormand (Dvrklln): but I've still used alt accounts and gotten to master quickly
[2018-03-08 00:20] http404error: I assume you construct an army using point-spend, right?
[2018-03-08 00:20] http404error: How's the point cap set?
[2018-03-08 00:20] lormand (Dvrklln): anyway example of a piece someone suggested that is clearly not balanced
[2018-03-08 00:21] lormand (Dvrklln): 80 at beginner, up to 100 at master
[2018-03-08 00:21] http404error: Not only unbalanced but hideously complicated and hard to explain
[2018-03-08 00:21] lormand (Dvrklln): and that's why pretendpiecing has a bad rep
[2018-03-08 00:21] http404error: It's the same in any community
[2018-03-08 00:21] wilson0862 (Wilson): pretendpiecing = creating units?
[2018-03-08 00:21] http404error: although in some cases a strong community culture can uplift everyone...?
[2018-03-08 00:22] http404error: Which is really weird to think about
[2018-03-08 00:22] http404error: It's so hard to explain things like "why simplicity is important" to someone who doesn't already have that idea in their head
[2018-03-08 00:22] http404error: I think in the case of Custom MTG cards it's because the makers of the game write so much about it all the time
[2018-03-08 00:22] http404error: so you can just cite the writing at nubs
[2018-03-08 00:23] http404error: in other communities I think the leadership isn't as strong, so everyone has to be tolerant of poor designs and the baseline expectation doesn't move upward
[2018-03-08 00:23] lormand (Dvrklln): anyway that clip of spiritfryer and ssp that I talked about earlier is part of a video by main_gi which he doesn't really want me to share because it could be kind of offensive to someone since it's a meme video
[2018-03-08 00:24] http404error: somehow that makes it funnier
[2018-03-08 00:24] lormand (Dvrklln): I can pm it with a start at this time link to anyone who wants it so you can just get the mapbud link and pretend you didn't watch the rest
[2018-03-08 00:24] lormand (Dvrklln): which might not necessarily even make sense without knowing the community and history
[2018-03-08 00:25] ryanfourpm: I don't think I've ever actually seen it despite being in the community, could you send it to me?
[2018-03-08 00:25] mqp: dvorak in CEO, for unit costing, how good of a heuristic is just unit mobility (let's say cost = ax + by where x is the number of squares it can move to and y is the number of squares it can attack)
[2018-03-08 00:26] mqp: i'm not sure how else i would think about the relative value of units than that
[2018-03-08 00:26] mqp: other than particular opening lines perhaps
[2018-03-08 00:26] ryanfourpm: sometimes it works, for simple units
[2018-03-08 00:26] lormand (Dvrklln): video is mostly clips from adam vermeer, a youtuber who I think makes inspirational videos and stuff. He became a meme in the ceo community after the dev posted a cryptic message in the patch notes about "the grandest scheme activated", and googling the grandest scheme turns up the video made by adam vermeer used in the meme video
[2018-03-08 00:26] lormand (Dvrklln): also listen to ryan he's #1 actual player
[2018-03-08 00:27] wilson0862 (Wilson): @Dvrklln PM and gib link :p
[2018-03-08 00:27] wilson0862 (Wilson): When will you be #1 player in Prismata too?
[2018-03-08 00:27] ryanfourpm: soon to be #1 player in prismata too
[2018-03-08 00:27] mqp: what kinds of other things are critical to unit cost?
[2018-03-08 00:27] mtanzer: at least wait until after our tourney match lol
[2018-03-08 00:28] wilson0862 (Wilson): Honestly I thought you were better as a new player than a new player should be. I briefly wondered if smurf.
[2018-03-08 00:28] http404error: #1 among actual players might not count for much judging by the others I've seen though 😛
[2018-03-08 00:28] ryanfourpm: the best units in CEO tend to have easy to use mobility
[2018-03-08 00:28] 307th: yeah ryan impressed in the golden cup for sure
[2018-03-08 00:28] lormand (Dvrklln): the highest ranked player is k218, who was found to have made a bot for the game
[2018-03-08 00:29] mqp: so drawing on chess as an example the obvious way i can understand is by comparing the relative value of a bishop and rook
[2018-03-08 00:29] ryanfourpm: In CEO there are  a lot of pieces that just can't get anywhere very fast, and so are terrible
[2018-03-08 00:29] ryanfourpm: bishop and rook have awkward-ish mobility compared to lots of newer units
[2018-03-08 00:29] http404error: does ranking actually imply positive winrate against the other leaders?
[2018-03-08 00:30] ryanfourpm: so they are a bit below average
[2018-03-08 00:30] mtanzer: how did they find out he made a bot
[2018-03-08 00:30] lormand (Dvrklln): it's also interesting how in classic bishop and knight are usually valued equally, but in ceo knight is 6 and bishop is 9
[2018-03-08 00:30] ryanfourpm: there is tons of stuff, he plays like a bot

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-03-08) ---
[2018-03-08 00:30] http404error: how can someone who "plays like a bot" be topping the rankings, is my question then
[2018-03-08 00:30] ryanfourpm: I have found numerous glitches in his AI a normal human would never make
[2018-03-08 00:30] wilson0862 (Wilson): Plays beyond our current understanding of the game. 🤔;
[2018-03-08 00:30] lormand (Dvrklln): someone made what might have been a joke about it, and then people took it seriously and ryan helped by posting a few videos with k2 making mistakes that only an AI could make
[2018-03-08 00:30] mqp: ok so i'm dreaming of some mobility metric that is like "over all other squares, the average number of moves it takes you to get to any given one"
[2018-03-08 00:31] mqp: this metric would correctly indicate that rooks are much more mobile than bishops and knights
[2018-03-08 00:31] lormand (Dvrklln): I remember someone tried to make a value index based on movement
[2018-03-08 00:31] lormand (Dvrklln): it didn't work so well
[2018-03-08 00:31] http404error: I think it can be done
[2018-03-08 00:31] http404error: Usefulness will scale up with complexity of calculation though
[2018-03-08 00:31] wilson0862 (Wilson): As like all other models.
[2018-03-08 00:32] lormand (Dvrklln): it'd be interesting to see someone try again
[2018-03-08 00:32] http404error: Carry that out to infinity and you get a winrate simulator so..
[2018-03-08 00:32] http404error: maybe winrate is just the best metric 😛
[2018-03-08 00:32] mqp: but i feel that these metrics are obviously naive so far because in chess the key thing affecting the mobility of knights, bishops, and rooks in any given position is pawn structure
[2018-03-08 00:32] ryanfourpm: I've never tried to make a metric to calculate piece value in CEO, it seems like an impossible task
[2018-03-08 00:32] mqp: so if you don't understand pawn structures that arise in chess you can't really understand the values of the units
[2018-03-08 00:32] mqp: it must be the same in CEO with respect to some positional properties i can't understand because i don't play it
[2018-03-08 00:33] lormand (Dvrklln): also as for ranking showing superiority over other players, as you get higher ranked you need to win a much larger percentage of games
[2018-03-08 00:33] lormand (Dvrklln): the minimum rating gain gets extremely small while the loss gets giant
[2018-03-08 00:33] lormand (Dvrklln): what's the ratio again ryan?
[2018-03-08 00:33] wilson0862 (Wilson): I'm assuming ELO rating system still.
[2018-03-08 00:33] ryanfourpm: I get 2 rating per win, and lose 45 if I lose
[2018-03-08 00:33] wilson0862 (Wilson): Your K factor must be super wonky.
[2018-03-08 00:34] ryanfourpm: the rating system it pretty weird, for every 1000 rating you get your maximum rating gain decreases by one
[2018-03-08 00:34] wilson0862 (Wilson): If you can't maintain a 95.7% winrate, how good are you really? 🤔
[2018-03-08 00:35] http404error: Elo is a name, not an acronym
[2018-03-08 00:35] http404error: It sounds like the ranking system is broken and favors volume of games played slightly over winrate
[2018-03-08 00:36] ryanfourpm: why are you posting things I said which require context
[2018-03-08 00:36] mqp: are there any other names that anyone always assumes are acronyms because they are short but unfamiliar?
[2018-03-08 00:36] lormand (Dvrklln): because they're fun without context
[2018-03-08 00:36] mqp: i can't think of any off the top of my head
[2018-03-08 00:36] http404error: The trick is, most of them are actually acronyms
[2018-03-08 00:36] ryanfourpm: the rating system is actually okay, pretty much everyone has stabilized their rating
[2018-03-08 00:36] http404error: And most foreign-looking names are longer
[2018-03-08 00:36] wilson0862 (Wilson): ya but ELO is kind of standard/accepted way to type it out now. :p
[2018-03-08 00:36] http404error: like "Euler" is the closest one I can think of but no one mistakes that one afaik
[2018-03-08 00:37] lormand (Dvrklln): ok the context for that is this other amazing spirit clip
[2018-03-08 00:37] lormand (Dvrklln): https://clips.twitch.tv/PeacefulCrepuscularGoldfishCoolStoryBro
SpiritFryer Playing Chess - Twitch Clips
mpabdu gets mad at stratshotplayer - Clipped by mtgtcg
[2018-03-08 00:38] ryanfourpm: kinda, idk if spiritfryer will be though lol
[2018-03-08 00:38] lormand (Dvrklln): posting things out of context is the best way to post things
[2018-03-08 00:38] http404error: Anyways, the rankings would probably be greatly improved by just using a naive Elo implementation
[2018-03-08 00:39] ryanfourpm: yeah I think I could actually gain rating infinitely in the current system, if I put in the time
[2018-03-08 00:40] mqp: it's a pretty weird crank signal to implement some bespoke rating system
[2018-03-08 00:40] mqp: if someone does that you know they reinvented every single wheel that they could get their hands on
[2018-03-08 00:40] lormand (Dvrklln): once you reach escape velocity yeah
[2018-03-08 00:40] http404error: Probably just naivete
[2018-03-08 00:40] lormand (Dvrklln): there's a convenient piecemaker with all the moves put in
[2018-03-08 00:40] lormand (Dvrklln): he uses the custom moves feature to take existing moves and abbreviate them in his notation
[2018-03-08 00:41] mqp: like i fully expect to go look at the CEO codebase and find some file that implements a special PRNG that the author thinks is the most random PRNG or something

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-03-08) ---
[2018-03-08 00:41] lormand (Dvrklln): lol we're currently working on datamining it
[2018-03-08 00:42] mqp: sincere question, do strong CEO players really like playing with this grid that shows what squares are attacked and defended?
[2018-03-08 00:42] lormand (Dvrklln): well most of them yes
[2018-03-08 00:43] lormand (Dvrklln): however some don't at higher levels
[2018-03-08 00:43] lormand (Dvrklln): and ryan was slightly meming about how it's guaranteed to help your winrate if you remove the grid
[2018-03-08 00:43] lormand (Dvrklln): however if I remember correctly when spirit did that on stream he messed up a few times because of it
[2018-03-08 00:44] http404error: I mean, I can play Prismata with enemy supply hidden, but why would I choose to?
[2018-03-08 00:44] ryanfourpm: I don't play with the grid, and I'm certain relying on it will stop you from improving
[2018-03-08 00:45] http404error: Doesn't it just help you track things you should already know?
[2018-03-08 00:45] mqp: i'm not sure, but assuming that all the movement is already loaded into your subconscious,  it feels like it would be really distracting to be seeing that grid when you are trying to calculate
[2018-03-08 00:45] ryanfourpm: I think paying attention to the grid is bad, you should just know how the pieces move
[2018-03-08 00:48] ryanfourpm: the only thing the grid really does is help prevent you from making one move blunders, and once you reach a certain level that shouldn't be a concern
[2018-03-08 00:53] http404error: It sounds like matchup would be a pretty huge factor, even just based on your backline.
[2018-03-08 00:54] http404error: Maybe I should go play the game to figure it out 😛
[2018-03-08 00:54] mqp: yeah i wonder ryan how much of the game do you think is decided in deck construction and how much is decided in game?
[2018-03-08 00:54] http404error: Instead of just blindly theorycrafting and expecting everyone else to hold my hand through it 😛
[2018-03-08 00:54] ryanfourpm: there is some mild luck involved in how your setup matches up against the other setup
[2018-03-08 00:55] ryanfourpm: also, knowing how to properly make a good setup is very important
[2018-03-08 00:56] ryanfourpm: it is hard to say how important setup construction exactly is
[2018-03-08 00:56] http404error: I really like the idea of a simple turn-based tactics game with no rng, it sounds really fresh
[2018-03-08 00:56] mqp: can't tell if sarcasm
[2018-03-08 00:56] http404error: I've always thought about tactics games as working downward from Warhammer
[2018-03-08 00:56] http404error: rather than upward from Chess
[2018-03-08 00:57] http404error: XCOM is a pretty popular game but so RNG-heavy
[2018-03-08 00:57] wilson0862 (Wilson): Ryan, you teach me how to be the #2 player in CEO and I'll teach you how to be the #30 player in Prismata or something.
[2018-03-08 00:57] wilson0862 (Wilson): Am I even #30 in a 20 player game? Who knows.
[2018-03-08 00:57] crash_overlord: you're not even in the top 100
[2018-03-08 00:58] mqp: ah i see "tactics game" was doing all the work in that sentence
[2018-03-08 00:58] wilson0862 (Wilson): Shh, Ryan doesn't know that @Crash_overlord
[2018-03-08 00:58] http404error: Yes, sorry that wasn't clear enough
[2018-03-08 00:58] mqp: you know i never made the connection between "turn-based tactics" in the video game sense and chess but now i admit they are somehow the same kind of game
[2018-03-08 00:58] http404error: Yeah, that was the big eye-opener for me from today
[2018-03-08 00:58] ryanfourpm: I feel like I'm way way way closer to top 30 in prismata than you are to #2 in CEO, hmm
[2018-03-08 00:58] wilson0862 (Wilson): Oh it's definitely a terrible trade for you.
[2018-03-08 00:59] http404error: Chess has a super big focus on "move to attack" that tactics games lack though
[2018-03-08 00:59] ryanfourpm: good negotiation session
[2018-03-08 00:59] wilson0862 (Wilson): I don't actually want to play CEO though so if you say no, I win anyway.
[2018-03-08 00:59] ryanfourpm: good point, I could force you to become part of CEO
[2018-03-08 01:00] crash_overlord: can you make me a CEO?
[2018-03-08 01:00] 307th: anon is thinking streets ahead
[2018-03-08 01:00] mqp: top 30 in prismata is surprisingly difficult now that everyone is playing
[2018-03-08 01:00] wilson0862 (Wilson): ya, I used my whole timebank on turn 1, 307th. I'm learning from the best.
[2018-03-08 01:00] crash_overlord: I was slowly falling today

[2018-03-08 01:37] http404error: It means the game's super hard and complex and nuanced, if that can happen without true luck
[2018-03-08 01:37] 307th: in prismata if you get a lead you typically win, whereas in chess if i was up against a 2100 rated player and i got up a piece it wouldn't necessarily matter
[2018-03-08 01:37] 307th: yeah i don't think it's a bad thing
[2018-03-08 01:37] 307th: it's just an outcome of the game being snowball-y
[2018-03-08 01:38] mtanzer: I thought it had more to do with the randomness of the sets
[2018-03-08 01:38] mqp: you know i'm not sure that snowbally is the right word for that even
[2018-03-08 01:38] crash_overlord: But it means the same thing kinda
[2018-03-08 01:38] mqp: it's more like in prismata each turn only moves the game state a little distance whereas in chess somehow you have the freedom to totally tank your position at any moment
[2018-03-08 01:38] mtanzer: although then again I imagine it's a lot harder to beat a 2100 chess player in chess960 than the analogous thing in pris?
[2018-03-08 01:39] 307th: prismata's super snowbally
[2018-03-08 01:39] 307th: remember, the slowest part of the game is the opening
[2018-03-08 01:39] mqp: chess ability and chess960 ability have like 0.9 correlation, they are only barely different skillsets
[2018-03-08 01:39] 307th: where you're buying drones that cost 3 gold and make 1 gold per turn
[2018-03-08 01:39] 307th: after that the ROI only gets higher
[2018-03-08 01:40] 307th: because of that, if a higher rated player makes one mistake on turn 3, and then the lower rated player makes a mistake on turns 6, 7, 8, and 10, the higher rated player may still lose
[2018-03-08 01:40] crash_overlord: Also similar to chess you can totaly tank your rating in 1 turn
[2018-03-08 01:40] crash_overlord: it just cant happen every turn
[2018-03-08 01:40] crash_overlord: or well, it actually can
[2018-03-08 01:40] crash_overlord: blunders occur in mata aswell
[2018-03-08 01:40] 307th: idk, blunders are pretty rare in prismata
[2018-03-08 01:40] crash_overlord: underdeffending centurion by 1 and getting it exact breached vs chill
[2018-03-08 01:41] crash_overlord: They're rare but happen
[2018-03-08 01:41] 307th: and top players only make them when they're pushing for an edge, whereas if they're comfortably ahead they can play it safe
[2018-03-08 01:41] mqp: in chess there is a really cool thing, chess query language, where you can interrogate a chess database for positions and games matching a great variety of criteria
[2018-03-08 01:42] mqp: like you can look for games where there were two queens on both sides simultaneously, or games where the same knight ended up moving to every corner of the board, or whatever you please
[2018-03-08 01:42] mqp: if there were a tool like that for prismata it would be real fun to look for weird plays and weird blunders that happened

messages.content: ```yeah I think some people like having new game mechanics for the same reason they like new cards in CCGs, because they feel bored of the same units/mechanics/cards after a little while.  I'm with you though, I prefer thinking hard about the current mechanics and understanding them and their consequences on a deeper level```

messages.author: awoodokehu
messages.content: It's unfortunate CEO is one of the only "really expanded" to a relative extent

messages.author: awoodokehu
messages.content: Because there are indeed new mechanics and while they are interesting (and the subjective issue that a chess opening is suddenly 30 or so moves of memorization) it doesn't make for a very functional system on the whole imo

messages.author: awoodokehu
messages.content: The game thankfully doesn't have as many problems as SSP's "pieces", CEO has some pieces that are already quite complicated and then trinket text is added on top to make it even more complicated, but not that many

messages.author: awoodokehu
messages.content: ```video is mostly clips from adam vermeer, a youtuber who I think makes inspirational videos and stuff. He became a meme in the ceo community after the dev posted a cryptic message in the patch notes about "the grandest scheme activated", and googling the grandest scheme turns up the video made by adam vermeer used in the meme video```

messages.author: awoodokehu
messages.content: I mean, what really makes the video a meme is that the dev himself commented about it and it's quite likely he named this after this video
